ety: ćarćā, q.v.+Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन् the termin. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment आपि
def: s.m. One who mentions or speaks (of), a mentioner; a reasoner ( = ćarćak).
s.m. Repeater; arguer.
s.m. A crumb.

ety: S. चट redupl.; or onomat.; cf. ćar
def: s.f. An imitative sound; prating, chatter, prate, gabble; scratching (of a pen in writing); creaking (of shoes); &c.
s.m.f. Prating, chattering ( = ćaṛ-baṛ); cracking (as burning wood, or a breaking branch), creaking (as shoes), &c.
adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Prating, talkative; — a prater, chatterer.
adj. (f. -ī), Hot, pungent, keen, sharp, acrid ( = ćarparā, q.v.).

s.f. Making a cracking sound, cracking (as burning wood, a branch breaking, cloth tearing, &c.), sputtering; creaking, &c.; pungency, keenness, sharpness (on the tongue); smarting, burning ( = ćarparāhaṭ).
v.n. To crack, to crackle, &c. ( = ćarćarānā, q.v.).

v.n. To chatter, prate, talk nonsense; to crackle (as burning wood), to sputter; to chew with a cracking noise; to creak (as shoes, &c.); to make a scratching noise (as a pen in writing); to be angry, peevish, or irritable, to fret, fume, storm; to be pungent or sharp (on the tongue); to smart, tingle, burn ( = ćarparānā).
s.f. = ćarćarāhaṭ, q.v.
s.m. A chatterer, prater.
s.m. = ćarćak, q.v.
s.f. Repetition of words in recitation.
v.t. To consider, reflect, apprehend, conceive; to peruse carefully,
s.m. An imitative sound (as of a cart-wheel, &c.); — idle prattle (cf. ćaraḵẖ-ćūṅ).

ety: for ćahār-dah; Z. ćathru-daśan; S. ćatur-daśan
def: adj. Fourteen; fouteen days old, full (the moon).

ety: for ćahār-dahum; Z. ćathru-daśa; S. ćatur-daśa
def: adj. Fourteenth; — the full moon.
s.m. A kind of falcon or hawk; a hyena.
s.m. A cricket.
adj. & s.m. Sordid, mean, low, vile, wicked, villainous; — a low or mean fellow; a villain; — a squint-eyed man
adj. & s.m. Sordid, mean, low, vile, wicked, villainous; — a low or mean fellow; a villain; — a squint-eyed man
s.f. Rise, ascent; increase, &c. ( = ćaṛhāʼo, which is the more com. word); attack, assault, onset; — alluvial land, alluvium (cf. ćar).
adj. (f. -ī), Risen, high (as prices, a tone, &c.); braced; swollen (as a river), &c.; in practice, in working order; in hand (as a job, &c.)
subs: s.m. A wealthy or great person

v.n. To be risen, &c.; to be in practice, to be in good working condition (as the hand, eye, &c.); to be in hand (as a work), to be in course of preparation; to be possessed (by an evil spirit; e.g. uske sir jin ćaṛhā-huʼā hai).

def: s.m. A rider, a trooper, &c. ( = ćaṛhait, q.v.).

s.f. Ascent; rise; ascension, &c. ( = ćaṛhāʼo); — conveying, conveyance; — embarkation; attack, assault, invasion, irruption; — price or fare for ascending, or riding, or embarking, &c., fare; passage-money.

ety: ćaṛh(nā)+ait = āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S.आपयन् (the termin. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment आपि)
def: s.m. A mounter, rider, horseman, trooper; an expert horseman.
s.m. A rider, &c. ( = ćaṛhait); — a trooper who does not ride his own horse.
s.m. = ćaṛhwaiyā, q.v.
s.m. Ascending; riding &c.

v.t. To cause to ascend, or mount, or embark, &c.; to cause to rise, or increase, or swell, &c.; to raise, lift, take up; to advance, elevate, exalt, magnify; to offer up (oblations or sacrifice), to sacrifice, devote; to brace (a drum, &c.); to string (a bow); to bend; to pull, draw on or up; to cock; to put (on, upon, or into, -par, — as a utensil on a fire, or colour on a thing, or goods into a boat or ship, &c.); to spread, to apply (colour, or decorations, to, -par, cloth, &c.); to fix (a bayonet, &c.); to put in hand (a work); to raise to the mouth, to drink, imbibe; to lead (an army, against, -par)

s.m. Ascent, rise, acclivity; rise (in prices, &c.); increase, augmentation; elevation, ascension; exaltation, promotion, advancement; rank, dignity; offering (made to idols = ćaṛhāwā); (in Math.) raising to a power, involution; swelling, inundation; flood-tide; up-stream; attack, assault, onset; access, fit; excitement, passion; loftiness, pride, conceit; intoxication; — string (of a bow)
subs: s.m. Ascent and descent; rise and fall; flood-tide and ebb-tide; up-hill and down-hill; up-stream and down-stream; ups and downs; (in Math.) involution and evolution; (in Mus) rising and descending scale.

def: s.m. A kind of jacket (worn by harlots).
adj. & adv. Capable of being ascended, or climbed, &c.; — in advance (as payment, &c.).
s.m. = ćaṛhauwā, q.v.
s.m. = ćaṛhauwā, q.v.
s.m. A shoe with a high heel (in contradistinction to a ghetlā, or a slipper without a heel).
s.m. Anything presented in sacrifice, a religious offering or gift; a present (from a bridegroom to his bride)
subs: To present or offer up a religious offering.

def: s.m. A shoe with a high heel (in contradistinction to a ghetlā, or a slipper without a heel).
s.f. = ćaṛhnī, q.v.

adv. With progressive or steady rise or increase; with raised voice, in a loud voice, aloud, in a high tone; confessedly, avowedly, designedly; in a superior manner or degree, excellently; — ćaṛh-ćaṛh-kar, adv. intens. of and = ćaṛhkar
subs: v.n. To speak with raised voice, or in a high tone, &c.

def: adv. With progressive or steady rise or increase; with raised voice, in a loud voice, aloud, in a high tone; confessedly, avowedly, designedly; in a superior manner or degree, excellently; — ćaṛh-ćaṛh-kar, adv. intens. of and = ćaṛhkar
subs: v.n. To speak with raised voice, or in a high tone, &c.

ety: ćaṛh˚ = Prk. चड्ढ(इ), or चड्ड(इ) = S. उद्+शद(ति; i.e. उच्छदति)
def: v.n. To go up, ascend, mount, climb; to soar; to ride (on, -par); to go (on board), embark; to go, be taken or conveyed, be transported (to); to go (on), fit; to go forward, advance, proceed (as a procession); to come on (as fever, &c.); to rise (as a price, a river, &c.), to increase, swell; to accumulate, fall in arrears (as pay. &c.); to be enlarged, to spread, to grow; to be raised, elevated, exalted, promoted; to be offered (as a sacrifice); to be drawn on or up, be tucked up; to be strung (as a bow); to be braced (as a drum, &c.); to be fixed (as a bayonet, or an arrow); to be set (as a bone); to be put (on, or in, as colour; or a weight, &c. in a scale, &c.), to be applied, to be spread or laid (over); to be entered (in, as an account or item), be put down; to march (against), lead an attack (against); to invade; to enter (the mind); to enter (the system, or head, &c.) and take effect (as poison, intoxicants, fever, &c.); to be possessed (by an evil spirit); to be taken, be caught, be hooked, be entrapped; to be superior (to), to excel; to gain (as a watch); to pass, elapse (as time, e.g. pahar-bhar din ćaṛhā); to be put in hand, be taken in hand (as a work)
subs: (-par), To come (against), to be led to the attack (of)
v.n. To ascend and descend; to be advanced and lowered; to be hired and dismissed; — congressus
v.n. To advance, gain ground, to get the better (of), to win, score (a victory); to find an opportunity

def: s.f. Preparation for battle.

adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Ascending, rising, increasing, waxing (as the moon), advancing, progressing, progressive, growing; increasing in speed, accelerated; superior (to, -se), better, excelling; — rise or increase (of price, &c., see ćaṛhtī); settlement of revenue at a progressively increasing rate
subs: s.m. A rise, or an advance, in price
s.m. A lease for a term of years at a progressively increasing rent
v.n. To be rising, &c.; to be superior (to, -se), be better (than), to excel.

ety: ćaṛh(nā)+tī = atī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आपि caus. augment+ति+का (or आप्तिका)
def: s.f. Rise, increase, advance; superiority; advantage, profit, gain.
s.m. One who ascends or mounts; a climber; a rider, horseman; one who places goods or grain, &c. in the scale for weighment.
v.t. To cause to be raised, to have (a thing, &c.) raised sent up, pulled on, put on, &c. (see ćaṛhnā).
s.f. Raising rents.
s.f. Green corn cut for fodder; — a small portion of land held rent-free by a cultivator ( = ćarotar).
adj. Movable, locomotive; unsteady, shaking, tremulous.
s.f. The leg-trick (in wrestling), tripping.

part. & s.m. Gone; gone to, attained, gone over or through, done; enacted; practised; customary; — acting, doing, practice, behaviour, &c. ( = ćaritr, q.v.); a fixed institute, proper or peculiar observance; disposition of mind
subs: (ćarita+artha), adj. Attaining one's object or end, successful in an undertaking, successful; effected; satisfied ( = kr̤itārth).

def: s.m. Acting, behaving; behaviour, conduct, carriage, bearing, procedure, manner, way; habit, practice, custom, usage; acts, deeds, proceedings; arts, tricks, wiles; exploits, adventures; story, history, or account of any one's deeds or exploits; nature, temper, disposition; quality; talent
subs: (i.e. strīya ćaritra), s.m. Woman's ways; woman's wiles.

def: adj. & s.m. White-spotted (as from leprosy); — morbid whiteness of the skin, white leprosy; a slight wound, a cut, a scratch; — cauterization; — damage, loss; fraud, swindle.
s.m. = ćarkā, q.v.

ety: ćāra+kāṭā = kāṭ˚+ā = iʼā = iwā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. The man who cuts forage for cattle, elephants, &c.; elephant-keeper's mate.

s.m. A wheel (as of a water-mill, or of a well, &c.); a potter's wheel; a lathe; the celestial globe or orb, the sphere of the heavens, the heavens, the sky; — circular motion; turn; — fortune, chance; — a cross-bow; a bow in a very tense state; — a falcon (particularly a white one); a hyena
subs: s.m. The ethereal sphere; the heaven of the stars
s.m. The blue vault, the ethereal sphere; — ćarḵẖ-ě-at̤las, s.m. The crystalline sphere or heaven (where the Mohammadans imagine the throne of God is fixed)
s.m. A bowman; an expert archer

ćaraḵẖ-pūjā, s.f. A ceremony observed by the lower orders of Hindūs on the day when the sun enters Aries, for the expiation of their sins (they are suspended, by an iron hook thrust through the flesh of the back, to one end of a lever which is raised on the top of a high pole, and whirled round by means of a rope attached to the other end: this penance is often performed by proxy for those who are rich enough to pay for it)
v.n. To be turned (as on a lathe); to dress or adorn (oneself)
ćarraḵẖ-ćūṅ, s.f. The creaking of a wheel (cf. ćar-ćūṅ)
s.m. Revolving heaven; the sky, the firmament; changeable fortune
s.m. The celestial orb; the sky
adj. & s.m. Whirling, turning, revolving; — one who turns a wheel; a spinner
v.n. To be turned; to turn round, whirl, revolve, rotate; to circulate; to be formed into globules (as quicksilver, &c.); to be tested (by fire, as metals)
v.n. To wheel, turn round, revolve, to move or soar in a circle; to circulate (as the blood)

def: s.m. = ćarḵẖā, q.v.
s.m. A spinning-wheel; a reel; the axis of a pulley; — a nag; jade, garran; — adj. Thin, weak, poor, sorry; worn out, rickety (as a cart, &c.)
subs: ćarḵẖā-ponī, ćarḵẖā-o-pūnī, s.f. Spinning and carding
v.n. To become worn out, rickety, weak, old, &c.

def: s.m. A spinning-wheel; a reel; the axis of a pulley; — a nag; jade, garran; — adj. Thin, weak, poor, sorry; worn out, rickety (as a cart, &c.)
subs: ćarḵẖā-ponī, ćarḵẖā-o-pūnī, s.f. Spinning and carding
v.n. To become worn out, rickety, weak, old, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. (dialec.); see s.v. ćar.
s.f. = ćarḵẖī, q.v.
s.f. A spinning-wheel; a pulley; an instrument for separating cotton from the seed; — a globe; a kind of fire-works, a catharine-wheel; — a dumb-waiter
subs: s.f. A revolving lantern made of tale with pictures all round, a Chinese lantern (syn. fānūs-ě-ḵẖiyāl).

def: s.m. A leper.
s.m. Skin, hide, leather, parchment; a shield
subs: s.m. ćarm-patrī, s.f. A manuscript of skin or parchment
s.m. A fold of skin, a wrinkle
ćarm-ćaṭkā, s.m., ćarm-caṭikā, ćarm-ćaṭī, s.f. A bat
adj. Made of skin or leather, leathern; encased in leather; abounding in leather
adj. Covered with hides or skins; covered with leather, leathery.

def: s.m. Hide, &c. ( = ćarm); the hide on which a student sits (usually that of an antelope); bark.
s.m. = ćamār, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Having a hide or skin; covered with skin or parchment; made of leather, leathern; armed with a shield; — one armed with a shield, a shield-bearer.
adj. Made of leather, leathern
v.n. (dialec.) = ćalnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. चरणअं = S. चरणीयं, rt. चर्
def: v.n. To graze, pasture; to feed, to pick, to eat.
s.m. Trousers reaching to the knees, half-trousers (syn. jāngiyā)
v.n. (dialec.) = ćaṛhnā, q.v.
v.n. (dialec.) = ćaṛhnā, q.v.
s.f. A feeding trough; a manger; a stable.
adj. = ćāroṅ, q.v.s.v. ćār, 'four.'
s.f. The kernel of the Chironjia sapida (see ćār); — kernel of a nut (generally).
s.m. A life-rent grant.
s.f. See s.v. ćār, 'four.'
s.m. = ćarphar, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Hot, pungent, biting, acrid; — smart, sharp, &c. ( = ćarpharā).
adj. = ćarparā, q.v.
s.f. Pungency, sharpness; smarting, tingling, burning.
s.f. = ćarparāhat, q.v.
v.n. To smart, tingle, burn (as a wound, &c.).
v.n. = ćarparānā, q.v.
adj. Active, smart; expert, ingenious (cf. ćaṭpaṭiyā).
s.m. Quickness, activity, smartness; cleverness, expertness, dexterity, skill, ingenuity.
adj. (f. -ī), Quick, active, smart; clever, expert, dexterous, skilful, ingenious; attentive, vigilant ( = ćaraph).
v.n. (prov.) To move, shake, &c. = ḍolnā, q.v. (cf. ćatpaṭāṅā).

r: ćarpūj, ćarpūz, ćar, ćal, pūj, pūć
ety: ćar or ćal+P. pūj or pūć
def: adj. Irregular, scrawly; loose, weak, feeble; vile, mean; useless; stupid; miserly; wicked.

r: ćarpūz, ćarpūj, ćar, ćal, pūj, pūć
ety: ćar or ćal+P. pūj or pūć
def: adj. Irregular, scrawly; loose, weak, feeble; vile, mean; useless; stupid; miserly; wicked.

ety: v.n. fr. ćarrā-nā, fr. ćar, q.v.
def: s.m. Cracking, crackling; crack; thunder-clap.
v.n. = ćarrānā, q.v.

v.n. To make a cracking noise in breaking or tearing (as cloth, &c.), to crack, crackle; to burst with a report; — to throb; to smart, to ache; — v.t. To cause to tear or be torn ( = ćirānā), to tear.
s.m. Hide (of an ox or buffalo), a skin; dressed leather (particularly the thickest and strongest sole leather; — syn. adhauṛī)
subs: s.f. lit. 'As much land as can be covered by a hide'; as much land as can be irrigated by a pair of bullocks.

def: s.m. Men, people, race, nation; — ćarshaṇī, s.f. Name of the wife of Varuna.
s.m. One who is addicted to smoking the intoxicating drug called ćaras.
s.f. Drowsiness, nodding, a nap.
s.m. contrac. of ćār-tār, q.v.s.v. ćār, 'four.'
s.m. One who does not fast.
adj. & s.m. Agreeable, welcome, approved, esteemed, beloved, dear; pleasing, beautiful, lovely, pretty, elegant; — an agreeable person; a beautiful person, &c.
s.m. A pot, a cauldron (cf. next); an oblation of rice, barley, and pulse, boiled with butter and milk for present tion to the gods or manes
subs: s.m. The distribution of the ćaru or oblation by Brahmans.

def: s.m. A great eater.
s.f. An earthen pot.
adj. Fourth.
adj. Fourth.
adj. Fourth.
s.m. A large earthen pot (syn. maṭkā)
s.m. A quadruped ( = ćār-pāya); a pony
s.m. A grazier, tender or feeder (of cattle, &c.), herdsman, cowherd, shepherd.
s.f. Taking (animals) to graze; the wages of a herdsman in grain; price paid for grazing or pasturage
subs: (-kī), To tend (animals) when grazing; take (animals) to grass.

def: s.f. Taking (animals) to graze; the wages of a herdsman in grain; price paid for grazing or pasturage
subs: (-kī), To tend (animals) when grazing; take (animals) to grass.

def: s.m. = ćarwāhā, q.v.
s.m. A philosopher or sophist who holds materialistic or unorthodox opinions, a sceptic, an atheist.

ety: ćār, q.v.+wāṅ = Prk. अमओ or अवँओ; S. तम+कः
def: adj. Fourth ( = ćauthā, q.v.).
s.m. Chewing, masticating; tasting.
v.t. To cause to graze; to graze, &c. ( = ćarānā, q.v.).
s.m. = ćārwāk, q.v.
s.m. = ćarwāhā.
s.f. A beautiful woman; splendour; moonlight; intelligence.

s.f. Going about, wandering, roaming; visiting; performing, practising, engaging in; due and regular observance of all rites and customs; following the rules of studentship; practising religious austerities; practice, conduct, behaviour, deportment; custom, usage.
s.m. A species of bustard, Otis bengalensis (cf. ćarg).
s.f. Ploughing, cultivation

ety: ćās(nā)+ā = iʼā = iwā = Prk. इअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. A ploughman, cultivator, tiller, husbandman.
s.f. Shooting pain, throbbing pain; pain, ache; a stich.
v.n. To shoot with pain, to throb, palpitate, beat.
s.m. The blue jay, Coracias indica (syn. nīl-kanṭh).

ety: rt. of ćash-īdan, Zend ćash = S. चष्
def: part. adj. Tasting (of), having a flavour or smack (of; — used in comp., e.g. namak-ćash, 'tasting of salt').
s.m. A drinking-vessel, a goblet, bowl, cup, wine-glass; — spirituous liquor.
s.f. A tasting, a taste.
s.m. Decay, infirmity, feebleness.
s.m. = ćāsī; ćāsā, q.v.
s.f. Tasting (used in comp).

ety: perf. part. of ćash-īdan; see ćash
def: part. Tasted; experienced; having tasted; having experienced ( = ćakh-kar).

ety: Pehl. ćashm; Zend ćashman; S. ćaksh+man (cf. S. ćakshan; ćakshus)
def: v.n. To be looking out (for), to be expecting, to be waiting (for); to be impatient (for)
subs: adj. Kissing the eyes (by way of affectionate salutation); hanging on the eyes (as drops of water, or tears)
s.m.f. An eye that looks half-closed (from modesty, an epithet of beauty), a drooping or languid eye
adj. & s.m. Conniving or winking (at); — a winker, conniver

s.f. Turning away the eyes; affecting not to see or hear; conniving, connivance; overlooking, palliating, excusing; — ćashm-poshī karnā (-se), To turn the eyes away (from); to pretend not to see or hear; to overlook, to cover; to wink or connive (at), to palliate, to excuse
s.f. Moistness of eye; moist eyes, full eyes, weeping
s.f.m. Eyes tinged with blood, blood-shot eyes; fiery eyes, fierce look, murderous looks
s.f. Hope, expectation, desire; trust, confidence
s.m.f. Look of an evil eye; fatal misfortune (in consequence of witchcraft, &c.); a killing look; an eye-sore
adj. & s.m.f. Dearly beloved; — a beloved object; light of the eye
adj. Gazelle-eyed, fawn-eyed, soft-eyed (an epithet expressive of beauty).

def: s.m. = ćashma, q.v.
s.m. A spring, source, fount; a fountain; — spectacles ( = ćashmak); — a batten
subs: 'To wear silver spectacles'; (fig.) to take bribes.

def: s.f. Winking, a wink; looking askance (at), coldness, misunderstanding (e.g. merī un-se ćashmak hai); — spectacles; — the seed of Cassia absus (used as a remedy in diseases of the eye; syn. ćāksū)
subs: ćashmak-zanāṅ, adj. & s.m. Winking; — a winker

ety: fr. Zend ćaśh = S. चष्, cf. ćāsht
def: s.f. Taste, flavour, relish; a mixture of sweet and sour; a taste by way of a sample; a specimen; a piece of gold or silver melted to prove its purity; proof, trial; — syrup; the viscous state of a syrup
subs: s.m. A taster; a servant whose duty it is to watch over the kitchen of princes and to taste every dish brought on table (as a security against poison; cf. bakāwal).

def: s.f. = ćāsnī, q.v.
s.f. The middle hour between sunrise and the meridian; the meal eaten at that hour, breakfast; dinner, a collation
s.m. A ploughman, &c. ( = ćāsā, q.v.); — s.f. ćās(nā)+S. (i)tavyā+tā+ikā), Ploughing, &c. ( = ćās, q.v.)
s.m. Taste (for), relish; longing, ardent desire; prurience, itching; habit, custom
subs: ćaskā lagnā, ćaskā ho-jānā (-kā), To acquire or have a taste (for), &c.

def: v.t. To cause to pain or throb.

ety: ćās˚, prob. fr. S. चकर्ष˚, by redupl. fr. rt. कृष्
def: v.t. To plough, till, cultivate.
v.n. To burst or split (as tight cloth); to be torn or rent (syn. masaknā; phasaknā).
s.f. A large pan in which sugar-cane juice is boiled.

ety: imperf. part. of ćaspīdan = ćapsīdan = ćavsīdan = javsīdan, rt. javs = Zend yu = S. यु
def: adj. Sticking (to), adhesive, viscous, slimy; coherent; congruous; applicable, suitable, to the point
subs: v.t. To join or cause to stick (to, -par), to stick, affix (on, — as a paper or notice on a board or wall, &c.).

def: s.f. Sticking together, adhesiveness, viscosity.

ety: perf. part. of ćaspīdan; see ćaspān
def: part. adj. Stuck (to), glued, cemented, adhered; attached (to), inclined (to), addicted (to), fond (of), liking.
s.f. Adhesion, tenacity, coherence; attachment, love, affection.
s.f. A cutaneous disease peculiar to the palms of the hands and the soles of the feet (said to be occasioned by insects); a kind of leprosy.
s.m. A cheat, a rogue.
s.f. The sound of breaking, or snapping, or cracking; a snap, a crack; — a scratch, a slight cut or wound, an excoriation, a sore, a scab, a chancre
subs: s.f. The sound of repeated snapping or cracking, the sound of the crackling of a burning body, or of quickly repeated strokes, &c.; the sound made in cracking the fingers (cf. ćaṭā-caṭ)
v.n. To break with a snap or crack; to snap short.

ety: contrac. of ćāṭ; see ćāṭnā and ćaṭānā
def: s.f. Eating up, devouring, &c. (used chiefly in comp.); — adv. Wholly, altogether, clean
subs: s.m. Eating up the whole, licking the platter clean
(-ko), To eat with zest; to eat up entirely, to lick the platter clean, to devour, consume; to dissipate; to make away with, to embezzle
v.n. To be eaten up entirely, be swallowed up, be devoured, be consumed; to be dissipated, &c.

ety: v.n. of ćāṭnā, q.v.; and cf. P. ćāsht and ćāshnī
def: s.f. Eating, tasting, licking; lick; taste, flavour; relish; a delicacy, bonne bouche; — wish, longing, prurience; habit, custom
subs: (-kī), To get thetaste (of); to acquire a taste or longing (for)
(-ko), To give one a taste for; to inculcate a habit

def: adv. Quickly, instantly, in a trice
subs: adv. Quickly, instantly, hastily, in a hurry; abruptly, suddenly, all at once
ćaṭ-ā-paṭī, s.f. Quickness, agility; speed; haste, hurry; suddenness; sudden death; a number of deaths in rapid succession.

def: s.m. The vessel that receives the juice of the sugar-cane as it drops from the mill (cf. ćāṭī).

s.m. Repeated sound of snapping or cracking; the sound of snapping or cracking the finger-joints; the sound of quickly repeated strokes, &c. (see ćaṭ-ćaṭ, s.v. ćaṭ); — adv. Repeatedly, successively, in quick succession.

ety: Prk. चडक्किआ or चंडाइआ = S. चट+क+इका
def: s.f. A mat (so called from the cracking noise it makes)
subs: To spread a mat, to mat.

def: s.m. The pied cuckoo, Cuculus melanoleucus (syn. papīhā, living, according to the legend, only upon rain-drops).
s.m. (f. -ā), A sparrow.
s.f. (m.?), Brightness, brilliance, lustre, splendour; bright colour; glitter; gaudiness; prime of life
subs: adj. Bright, brilliant, glittering, gaudy ( = ćaṭkīlā)

def: adj. Quick, sharp, intelligent, shrewd; cunning, artful (also written ćaṭāḵẖ).

ety: Prk. चडक्की and चडक्कओ; S. चट+ क+का and कः
def: s.f. (intens.) Loud cracks or reports; repeated cracks, &c.; also = ćaṭ-ćaṭ, q.v.
s.f. Quickness, briskness, smartness; intelligence; — adj. Quick, sharp, intelligent
s.f. (m.?), A crack, snap, crash, a smack ( = ćaṭ)
subs: v.n. To pay with the foretop sail.

def: s.m. = ćaṭāḵẖ, or ćaṭāk, q.v.

ety: Prk. चडक्की and चडक्कओ; S. चट+ क+का and कः
def: s.f. (intens.) Loud cracks or reports; repeated cracks, &c.; also = ćaṭ-ćaṭ, q.v.
s.m. = ćaṭāḵẖ, or ćaṭāk, q.v.

ety: Prk. चडक्की and चडक्कओ; S. चट+ क+का and कः
def: s.f. (intens.) Loud cracks or reports; repeated cracks, &c.; also = ćaṭ-ćaṭ, q.v.
v.n. = ćaṭaknā, q.v.

ety: Prk. चडक्क(इ) or चडक्के(इ), fr. S. चट+कृ
def: v.n. To give forth a snapping or cracking sound, to crack, to crackle (as burning wood, &c.); to break, to crack with a report, to split, burst, explode; to burst or open (as a bud), to bloom; to be irritated; to speak snappishly, or angrily, or rudely; a split or dispute to occur, alienation or estrangement to take place (between, -meṅ, two persons, — in this sense, the verb, in the past imperfect tense, is idiomatically used in the feminine, e.g. un donoṅ-meṅ ḵẖūb ćaṭkī), to fall out; to make (oneself) scarce, to make off, to disappear, go away suddenly; (in Urdū the word is commonly written ćaṭaḵẖnā)
s.f. Quickness, &c. ( = ćaṭak; ćaṭ-paṭi).
s.f. Rock, rocky ground; a large block of stone.

v.t. To cause to taste; to cause to lick or lap; to put (a sword, &c.) to the grindstone, to sharpen, to whet (a sword, &c.); to give (one) a smack or taste of (a whip; or a douceur, or bribe, &c.); — s.m. The ceremony of feeding a child for the first time.
s.f. See s.v. ćaṭ, 'quickly.'
s.f. A variegated colour
s.m. A large net, a seine; — a conspiracy (syn. ćor-manḍalī).
s.f. A species of herb which springs up with the rabīʻ or spring crops. (It is used as fodder for cattle; and the poorer cultivators use the seeds, mixed with barley, for food.)
adj. (f. -ī), = ćaṭpaṭiyā, q.v.
v.n. To make a cracking or crackling noise, to make a snap or crash (as anything hard in breaking).
s.f. An earthen vessel or pitcher, a 'chatty'; a churn.
s.m. A school-boy, pupil, scholar ( = ćaṭṭā).

ety: S. चर्पट+इका+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To slap, box, cuff (see ćāṅṭā).
s.m. A parching sun; deficiency, scarcity, dearth, famine; excessive thirst; eager desire
s.m. A box on the ear, a slap, a thump, a cuff.

v.t. To snap (the fingers, or a gun, &c.), to crack (a whip, &c); to break with a snap, to split; to produce a cracking of the finger-joints (by bending the fingers); to fire off (a musket or gun); to irritate; to alienate; to drive away; to get rid of, dispose of.
s.f. Bolt (of a door).
s.m. A clack made by the tongue against the palate on tasting something pleasant or pungent
subs: v.n. To make a clacking noise with the tongue; to smack the lips (over anything luscious).

def: s.f. The clack of the tongue in urging cattle, &c.; the cracking noise made by the finger-joints (when bent and pressed).
v.t. To clack the tongue against the palate (on tasting anything luscious or pungent); to urge cattle by clacking the tongue ( = ṭiṭkārnā); to snap or crack the fingers (by bending them).
v.t. = ćaṭkānā, q.v.
s.f. = ćaṭkanī, q.v.
s.m. = ćaṭkārā, q.v.
v.t. = ćaṭkārnā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Bright, brilliant, glittering, splendid, glowing; gaudy, showy, gay
subs: savoury; strong, in excess (as salt, &c. in a dish).

ety: ćāṭ˚ = Prk. चड्ड(इ), fr. S. चष्ट. p.p.p. of rt. चष्
def: v.t. To lick; to lap; to taste, to eat (cloth, as a worm; or herbage, as a locust), to consume, devour
subs: (intens.), v.t. To lick up; to eat up, &c.

def: v.n. To be tasted, &c. (see ćāṭnā); — adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Voracious; — a voracious person.

s.f. A kind of pickle or sauce (the solid ingredients of which are chopped or bruised and retained with the vinegar); a mixture of salt and pepper with some acid for a relish; seasoning, condiment, relish; — state of being cut to pieces, or mangled, or bruised, or mashed, &c.
subs: (-ko), To reduce to a state of ćaṭnī, to reduce to pulp, to bruise, mash, mangle, &c.
v.n. To be reduced to pulp, be bruised or mashed, &c.; to be eaten with relish, be quickly devoured.

def: s.m. = ćaṭokhā, q.v.
s.m. The speckled dove.

ety: ćāṭ, q.v.+Prk. उल्लो and उल्लओ; S. उल and उल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Greedy, voracious; toothsome; — a gourmand; an epicure (syn. ćaṭnā); one who wastes his money on delicacies.

ety: ćāṭ, q.v.+Prk. उल्लो and उल्लओ; S. उल and उल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Greedy, voracious; toothsome; — a gourmand; an epicure (syn. ćaṭnā); one who wastes his money on delicacies.
s.m. Toothsomeness, epicureanism.
s.m. Toothsomeness, epicureanism.
adv. See s.v. ćaṭ; and cf. jhaṭ-paṭ.
adj. (f. -ī) = ćaṭpaṭiyā.
s.f. Knocking or tossing about, floundering, fluttering; flutter, flurry; palpitation, agitation, distress.
v.n. To flutter, flounder, knock about, toss about; to be agitated; to palpitate; to wince; to dance about (as frying grain), to be frying.
s.f. Quickness, briskness, &c.; see s.v. ćaṭ.
adj. Quick, active, brisk, smart, alert; hasty, precipitate; expert, adroit, clever; — hot, pungent; tasty, savoury ( = ćarparā).
s.m. An umbrella, a parasol
subs: ćatar-mār, s.m. A toadstool; a mushroom.

ety: catur, q.v.+Ap. Prk. अउ = Prk. अअं = अत्तं = S. क+त्वं
def: s.m. = ćaturāʼī, q.v.
s.f. A species of herb which springs up with the rabīʻ or spring crops. (It is used as fodder for cattle; and the poorer cultivators use the seeds, mixed with barley, for food.)
s.f. An umbrella, a parasol; a veil, a canopy; a tent, a pavilion.
s.f. A school (syn. pāṭh-śālā).
s.f. A school (syn. pāṭh-śālā).
s.f. A school (syn. pāṭh-śālā).
s.m. An excoriation; a red spot, a pimple; &c. ( = ćaṭ, q.v.); — calumny, slander; a stroke or blow of misfortune, a loss ( = ćaṭṭī); — a heap, pile (of bricks, &c.).
s.m. A schoolboy, a pupil.
s.m. A child's toy, consisting of a number of small (painted) wooden balls suspended from a flat or saucer-shaped piece of wood, which rattle when shaken
subs: v.n. To juggle with cups and balls; to carry tales which set people by the ears.

def: s.m. A delicacy, dainty, tit-bit, bonne-bouche.
s.f. Diminution; deficiency, lack, scarcity, want; loss (in trade, &c.), injury, damage ( = ćaṭṭā; ćaṭkā); — expense, charge
subs: (-kī), To make good a loss; to suffer a loss; to incur expense, to pay (for).

def: s.m. Pleasing or grateful words or discourse, flattery, wheedling, coaxing.
adj. & s.m. Toothsome; greedy; — am epicure ( = ćaṭorā); — a gourmand; — one who takes bribes.
s.m. A kind of plaything (cf. ćaṭṭā-baṭṭā); — a species of hawk.
adj. Relating to four
subs: adj. Suited to the four tribes or castes; belonging to the four castes; — s.m. The aggregate of the four original castes, viz. the Brāhman, Kshatriya, Vaiśya, and Śūdra ( = ćātur-varṇ)
adj. & s.m. Versed in or familiar with the four Vedas; — one so versed ( = ćaube, q.v.); — knowledge of the four Vedas.

def: adj. Four ( = ćatur, q.v.)
subs: s.m. The country of the four castes, India.

def: adj. Clever, able, accomplished, shrewd, sagacious; skilful, dexterous ( = ćatur).
adj. & s.m. Swift, quick, active, smart; expert, dexterous, clever, skilful, ingenious, adroit, sharp, shrewd, knowing, sagacious, wise; cunning, astute, sly; — a shrewd person, &c.

ety: changeable in comp. to ćatus, and ćatush
def: adj. Four
subs: adj. & s.m. Four-cornered, quadrangular; — a quadrangle; a quadrangular figure, a square

adj. & s.m. Consisting of four members or parts, quadripartite; — an entire or complete army, comprising elephants, chariots, cavalry, and infantry; — the game of chess (cf. P. shatranj); — a kind of song; a variety of music
adj. & s.m. Having four faces; — an epithet of Brahma
adj. & s.m. Having four arms; four-sided, quadrilateral; — an epithet of Vishnu; — a four-sided figure, a square
s.f. 'The four-armed one,' an epithet of Durgā
s.m. A place where four roads meet, a cross-way
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Having four feet; consisting of four pādas; — a quadruped; anything which has four parts or members; a metre or stanza consisting of four padas or lines
s.f. The fourteenth day of the moon's age
adv. Towards the four quarters, on all sides, all around
adj. & s.m. Quadrangular, square; — a quadrangular figure, a tetragon, a square ( = ćau-koṇ)
adj. & s.m. Having four faces; — the four-faced one, an epithet of Brahma
s.m. The four objects of human pursuit collectively, viz. dharm, 'virtue'; kām, 'pleasure'; arth, 'wealth'; and moksh, 'final beatitude'
s.m. The four classes or castes of Hindūs, viz. the Brāhman, Kshatriya, Vaiśya, and Śūdra
adj. & s.m. Familiar with the four Vedas; — one so familiar ( = ćaube); — ćatur-yug, adj. & s.m. Containing the four yugas; — the aggregate of the four ages or epochs of the world ( = ćau-jug).

def: adj. = ćatur, q.v.; — s.m. A wise, or shrewd, or clever man, &c.
s.f. Alertness; expertness, dexterity, smartness, cleverness, knowledge, wisdom, sagacity, skilfulness, ingenuity; astuteness, cunning, slyness, subtlety, craftiness, deceit, roguery.
adj. = S چتردش चतुर्दश ćatur-daś, adj. See s.v. ćatur.
s.f. = ćaturāʼī, q.v.
adj. = S چترسره चतुरस्र ćatur-asra, adj. See s.v. ćatur.
s.f. = ćaturāʼī, q.v.
s.f. = S چاتريه चातुर्य ćāturya, s.m. Cleverness, ability, shrewdness, skilfulness, dexterity ( = ćaturāʼī).
adj. Fourth ( = ćauthā)
subs: (ćaturtha+aṅśa), s.m. A fourth part, a quarter; receiving a fourth.

def: s.f. The fourth day in a lunar fortnight; the fourth (or dative) case (in Gram.)
subs: (ćaturthī+anta), adj. Terminating with the suffix or sign of the fourth (or dative) case.

def: s.m. = S چاتري चातुरी ćāturī, s.f. Cleverness, ability, shrewdness, skilfulness, dexterity ( = ćaturāʼī).
s.m. A hole in the ground (especially one dug to receive a burnt-offering).
v.t. = ćaṭānā, q.v.
adj. Bare, bleak, desolate

ety: ćā = ćāh˚, q.v.+ū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. Fond, loving; desirous, eager.

ety: prob. fr. Prk. चव्व = S. चर्व्
def: s.m. A grinder, a back-tooth; — a proughshare; a clump round a ploughshare.
adj. Four (used only in comp.)
subs: ćav-annī, s.f. The fourth part of a rupee, a four-anna piece; — ćau-annī-kā, adj. A quarter, a four-anna (share, &c.)

ćau-bāćhā, s.m. A levy of revenue on four things under the ancient regime in the Dehli territory, viz. the pāg or 'turban,' the tāg or 'string or thread worn by a child round the waist,' the kūṛī or 'hearth,' and pūṅchī or 'tails of cattle' (the first two correspond to the 'poll-tax,' the third to 'hearth-money' — the fumage of Domesday Book)
s.f. Wind blowing from the four quarters, &c. ( = ćau-wāʼī, q.v.)
s.m. A gusset under the arm-pit; a kind of jacket not open under the arms
s.m. The four fastenings, four joints; four limbs or members; — adj. Sound in the four limbs, vigorous, &c. (used in comp. with ćāq)
adj. & s.f. Of or appertaining to the four limbs or the four legs; — fastenings (of baggage, &c.); putting four new shoes on a horse or beast, shoeing; — ćau-bandī bāṅdhnā, To shoe (a horse or beast)
Bleeding in four limbs, viz. the arms and legs
s.f. An obsequial rite performed on the fourth anniversary of a death
s.m. A verse of four lines; — a play; — ćau-bolā ćhand, s.m. A kind of metre
adv. On all sides, all round, around
ćau-pattrī, s.f. A small tract, an essay, a pamphlet; a pocket-book
adj. & adv. Consisting of four watches, four-watch; — for or during four watches or twelve hours
ćau-pahlū, adj. & s.m. Four-sided, quadrilateral; — a four-sided figure, a quadrilateral; — ćau-pahal, s.f. A square bottle
adj. & s.m. Having four wheels, four-wheeled; — a four-wheeled vehicle or carriage
adj. & adv. All round, on all sides; — ćau-pher nāp, s.f. Perimeter
ćau-tārā, adj. & s.m. Having four strings or wires, four-stringed; — a four-stringed musical instrument
cau-tālā, s.m. A mode in music, the time of which is marked by four claps of the hands
s.m. A mitre; — a kind of tent with four ropes
adj. & s.f. Consisting of four strands; — a thread of four strands; — a kind of fishing line (not used with a rod)
ćau-taʼī, s.f. A piece of cloth of two breadths of double length
adj. Of or relating to the four ages or great periods; of vast antiquity
adj. Four-bordered, having an ornamental border on the four sides (a shawl, &c.)

adj. & s.m, (f. -ī), Of or appertaining to four boundaries, or environs; — the four boundaries; environs, surrounding country, neighbourhood; — a raised mound indicating where the boundaries of four villages meet
ćau-dāniya (fr. dānā), s.f. An ear-ring formed (usually) of four pearls
ćau-diś, ćau-diśā, adv. On all sides, all round
adj. 'Having four teeth or tusks'; crossing the tusks (as elephants do in fighting); — stout, sturdy, robust; bold, valiant
s.f. Stoutness, sturdiness; boldness, valour, heroism
s.m. A kind of sedan with two poles ( = ćau-pālā)
ćau-rāhā, s.m. Junction of four roads, cross-road, quadrivium; — ćau-rāhe-meṅ, adv. On the cross-road, in public
adj. (f. -ī), Of four colours; of four kinds
ćau-singhā, adj. Four-horned; — s.m. A raised mound indicating the junction of the boundaries of four villages ( = ćau-ḥadda)
s.m. (f. -ī), A ring of two pearls worn in each ear
ćau-konā, ćau-kor, adj. & s.m. Four-cornered, quadrangular, square; — a square
ćau-khūṅṭā, adj. & s.m. Four-cornered, quadrangular; rhomboidal, lozenge-shaped; — a four-sided figure, a quadrilateral
s.m. The four quarters of the globe, the world; — adv. On all four sides, all round
s.m. A rhombus; — ćau-khūṅṭ do barābar bāzū, s.m. A rhomboid
adv. On all sides, all round; — s.m.pl. Neighbourhood, adjacent or surrounding district or territory
adj. Four-cornered, &c. ( = ćau-koṅ); — s.m. A kind of oblong tray
s.f. A four-sided cap; — ćau-goshī, ćau-goshiyā, s.m. lit. 'Having four ears'; an animal (particularly a horse) with slit ears

ćau ghaṛā, adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Having four partitions or compartments, &c.; — a small box (of gold or silver, &c.) with four partitions (for holding spices or perfumes); — an earthen toy consisting of four small cups joined together
s.f. A raft or float supported on four or more earthern pitchers
s.m. A large well (admitting of four lāʼos or ropes being let down at one and the same time)
s.m. = ćau-laṛī, s.f. A necklace of four strings or rows
s.m. Four months; lands tilled during the four months of the rainy season and prepared for the rabīʻ sowing
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to the four months of the rains; — the four months (from Asaṛh to Kuʼār) constituting the rainy season; a kind of song (descriptive of the season) sung during the rains
ćau-masiya, s.m. A ploughman hired for the four months of the rainy season
ćau-maḥlā, adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Having four stories, four-storied; — a four-storied house, &c.
adj. & s.m. Having four kernels; — a walnut; — a large-headed person
adj. & s.m. Having four faces; having four points (an arrow, &c.); having four burners (a lamp); — a name of Brahmā; — a lamp-stand with four partitions or burners
adj. & s.m. Four-faced, &c. ( = ćau-mukh); — a kind of paṭā or fencing stick
v.n. To be good at argument
adj. = ćau-mukhā; — s.f. A name of the goddess Devī; — the seed of the Elœocarpus ganitrus
s.m. The junction of four streams, or of four roads
s.m. (orig.) A punishment which consists in tying the four outstretched limbs to four pegs; tying a man's arms and legs; tying the elbows behind; tying the four legs of a horse
adj. & s.m. Having four boundaries; — a place or village having four boundaries
s.f. Wind blowing from the four quarters, or from all quarters; — contrary winds; — a tempest, hurricane, commotion
s.m. A market where four roads meet, a square with shops on all sides.

def: s.f. See s.v. ćau.
s.m. = ćaube, q.v.
s.m. corr. of ćah-baćća, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. ćau.
s.f. A woman of the ćaube class; the wife of a ćaube.

ety: ćaub˚ = ćauw = ćau+ar = Prk. अडो = S. अ+रः
def: adj. Stout, sturdy, robust; bold.

ety: Prk. चोव्वारो or चउव्वारो, and चोव्वारओ, &c.; S. चतुर्वारः and चतुर्वार+कः
def: s.m. A shed; an assembly-room (of a village), a kind of summer-house or pavilion (generally built jointly by several people as an assembly-room or resting-place common to them all), a private room; a vestibule, a porch.

ety: Prk. चोव्वारो or चउव्वारो, and चोव्वारओ, &c.; S. चतुर्वारः and चतुर्वार+कः
def: s.m. A shed; an assembly-room (of a village), a kind of summer-house or pavilion (generally built jointly by several people as an assembly-room or resting-place common to them all), a private room; a vestibule, a porch.
s.m. A Brāhmaṇ acquainted with the four Vedas (now however the term is applied to the descendants of such, though not learned).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀯𑁂𑀤 (caüveda), 𑀘𑀉𑀯𑁂𑀬 (caüveya), 𑀘𑀉𑀯𑁆𑀯𑁂𑀤 (caüvveda) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit चतुर्वेदी (caturvedī).

def: adj. Twenty-four.
s.m. A tract of country containing twenty-four villages in the occupation of a particular tribe.
s.m. A tract of country containing twenty-four villages in the occupation of a particular tribe.
adj. (dialec.) = ćaudah, q.v.

ety: Prk. चउद्दहो and चउद्दहओ; S. चतुर्+ दशः and दश+कः
def: adj. Fourteen
subs: s.m. 'Receptacle of fourteen virtues'; one who possesses all qualities, a paragon of excellence
adj. The whole fourteen, the fourteen
s.m. The fourteen worlds or states of existence (viz. the earth, the six that are above it, and the seven that are below it)
s.m. The fourteen treasures that were produced at the churning of the ocean
s.f. The fourteen branches of learning taught in the system of the Hindūs.

def: adj. (f. -iṅ), Fourteenth.
adj. (f. -wīṅ), Fourteenth
subs: s.f. Fourteenth night (of the moon), the night on which the moon is full
Full moon; — a face like the full moon, a beautiful face.

def: s.f. See s.v. ćau
s.m. = ćaudhrī, q.v.

ety: Prk. चउद्दसी or चोद्दसी; S. चतुर्+दशी
def: s.f. The fourteenth day of the lunar fortnight.

ety: Prk. चउद्दसी or चोद्दसी; S. चतुर्+दशी
def: s.f. The fourteenth day of the lunar fortnight.
adj. = ćaudah-wāṅ, q.v.

ety: Prk. चउद्दहो and चउद्दहओ; S. चतुर्+ दशः and दश+कः
def: adj. Fourteen
subs: s.m. 'Receptacle of fourteen virtues'; one who possesses all qualities, a paragon of excellence
adj. The whole fourteen, the fourteen
s.m. The fourteen worlds or states of existence (viz. the earth, the six that are above it, and the seven that are below it)
s.m. The fourteen treasures that were produced at the churning of the ocean
s.f. The fourteen branches of learning taught in the system of the Hindūs.

def: adj. Stout, corpulent; sturdy, robust, vigorous, active (cf. ćaubar).

ety: Prk. चक्कधरी, and ˚धरअइआ and ˚धरआयंती; S. चक्र+धरी and ˚धर+ता+इका, and धर+आपयन्ती (the term. of the fem. pres. part. with the caus. augment आपि
def: s.f. The office, jurisdiction, dignity, or privileges, &c. of a ćaudhrī, q.v.

ety: Prk. चक्कधरी, and ˚धरअइआ and ˚धरआयंती; S. चक्र+धरी and ˚धर+ता+इका, and धर+आपयन्ती (the term. of the fem. pres. part. with the caus. augment आपि
def: s.f. The office, jurisdiction, dignity, or privileges, &c. of a ćaudhrī, q.v.

ety: Prk. चक्कधरी, and ˚धरअइआ and ˚धरआयंती; S. चक्र+धरी and ˚धर+ता+इका, and धर+आपयन्ती (the term. of the fem. pres. part. with the caus. augment आपि
def: s.f. The office, jurisdiction, dignity, or privileges, &c. of a ćaudhrī, q.v.
s.m. The head man of a caste, guild, profession, or trade; the head man of a village; a title, an honorific form of address.
s.m. A kind of sedan with two poles.
s.m. A plain (cf. ćau-gāna, s.v. ćau); — a game resembling hockey but played on horse-back, the game of polo; — the bat or stick with which the game is played
subs: s.m. A player of hockey on horseback, a polo-player
s.f. Playing the game of ćaugān
s.f. The place where ćaugān is played, polo-ground.

def: s.f. The straight tube of a ḥuqqa, q.v.
s.m. A rabbit; a hare.
adj. = ćau-guṇā, q.v.s.v. ćau.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀼𑀡 (caügguṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit चतुर्गुण (cáturguṇa). First attested in 1639 as Middle Hindi چوگنا (cvgna /⁠caugunā⁠/). By surface analysis, چَوـ (cau-, “four”) + ـگُنا (-gunā, “-fold”).

def: s.m. Name of a caste of Rājpūts (so called after their first king, ćatur-vāhu).
adj. (f. -ī), Having four folds; four-fold; consisting of a set of four.
adj. Seventy-four.

s.m. A quadrangle; a square; a court-yard; an open place in a town where the market is held (and where also the chief of the police-office is stationed); a daily market; the main street or central thoroughfare of a city; — a square place filled (at marriages and other occasious of rejoicing) with sweetmeats (which, after certain ceremonies, are distributed); — an ornamented square of coloured meal, &c., in which a bride and bridegroom are seated a short while during a certain number of nights before the wedding; the number four, an aggregate of four (e.g. tīn ćauk bārhā); — a grinder or back-tooth (being one of a set of four)
subs: ćauk pūrnā, To form a square space of coloured meal in which at marriages the bride and bridegroom are seated on chairs, &c.; — to fill a square space with sweetmeats, &c. on some occasion of rejoicing; — to make chequers or squares
s.m. A seat on which people sit and eat in the ceremony of ćauk
ćauk-ćāṅdnī, s.f. A festival held on the fourth of the light fortnight in Bhādoṅ, when the pupils of pātśālas worship Ganeś
v.n. A market to be held; a row of shops to be opened
s.m. A tax on all goods sold in a ćauk.

def: s.m. An aggregate of four, a four (e.g. do ćaukā aṭṭhā); — a square piece of ground; the space in which a Hindū cooks and eats his victuals (the ground being first plastered with mud and cowdung); a square slab of marble or other stone; — the four front teeth; — the cube of the measure called bāṅs
subs: To make a ćaukā (i.e. to plaster and prepare a space for cooking) and scrub the pots and pans.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀓𑁆𑀓 (caükka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka).

def: adj. (f. -ī), lit. 'Four-eared'; circumspect, cautious, vigilant, wary, on one's guard, alert, on the watch; sly
subs: (-se), To be on the alert, to be on one's guard (against), &c.

ety: ćauk, q.v.+aṛ = Prk. अडो = S. रः (with अ of base)
def: adj. Good, excellent, fine; strong, sturdy, stout.

adj. Cautious, careful, watchful, vigilant; alert, active; circumspect, diligent; intelligent, sharp, clever; — exact, accurate, proper, full (weight); — intj. ( = ćaukas rah) Be careful! be vigilant! &c.
subs: (-se), To be cautious or careful, to be vigilant, to be on one's guard (against), &c.
(-se), To make one cautious, &c., to put one on his guard, &c.

def: s.f. Caution, vigilance, watchfulness, watch, care, attention, circumspectness, circumspection; alertness; intelligence.
s.f. Watching, watch; vigilance, caution, circumspection, &c. ( = ćaukasāʼī, q.v.).
subs: (-ko), To keep watch (over), to watch, to guard; to be vigilant or circumspect (in respect of); to be on the alert.

def: s.m. An assayer, an examiner, a supervisor or inspector.
s.f. Four people employed together, or eating out of the same dish or plate.
s.m. = ćaukhaṭ, q.v.
s.m. A station where four boundaries meet.
s.m. The four pieces of wood forming the frame of a door ( = ćaukhaṭ).
s.m. The four pieces of wood forming the frame of a door ( = ćaukhaṭ).
s.m. The four pieces of wood forming the frame of a door ( = ćaukhaṭ).
adv. = ćaukudhan, q.v.
